modules.title: Kartläggning (A1.1–C1)
modules.desc: Adaptivt test som anpassar svårighet. Du får delnivå (A1.1, A1.2, A2.1 …) och en kort rapport.

grammar.q: There ___ a book on the table.
There ___ two books in the room.
There ___ a student on the table.
There ___ two students in the room.
There ___ a chair on the table.
There ___ two chairs in the room.
I have lived here ___ two years.
This test is ___ than the last one.

grammar.exp: Past continuous for ongoing past action.
Past simple with finished time (last night).
First conditional: if + present, will + verb.

grammar.q: That's the boy ___ lives next door.
If I ___ more time, I would travel.
grammar.exp: Second conditional: if + past, would + verb.
Reported speech often backshifts: is -> was.
Should have + V3 = criticism about past.

grammar.q: Hardly ___ I arrived when it started raining.
Not only ___ he finish on time, but he also helped others.
She speaks as if she ___ everything.
grammar.exp: As if + past for unreal present.

reading.text: My name is Sara. I am 15 years old. I live in Sweden with my family. I go to school every day. After school, I play games and eat dinner.

qs.q: What does she do after school?
qs.a: She plays games and eats dinner.

reading.text: Tom has a small dog. The dog is brown and very friendly. On Saturdays, Tom walks the dog in the park. The dog likes other dogs and runs a lot.

qs.q: When does Tom walk the dog?
Where does he walk the dog?

reading.text: Emma lives in London. She works at a bank near the river. She was born in 1998. On Mondays she starts work at 8:30. In the evening she studies English at home.

qs.q: What time does she start work on Mondays?

reading.text: Ali moved to a new city last year. At first, he felt nervous, but he joined a football club and met new friends. Now he feels more confident, and he practices English every evening.

qs.q: How did he meet new friends?

reading.text: Many students feel tired in the morning. One reason is that teenagers often need more sleep than adults. If school starts too early, students may struggle to focus, which can affect learning and mood.

qs.q: Why do many students feel tired?
qs.a: Because school starts too early and teenagers need more sleep.

qs.q: What can early school start affect?

reading.text: Social media can be useful for staying in touch, but it can also create stress. People may compare themselves to others and feel that they are not good enough. A healthy habit is to take breaks and focus on real-life activities.

qs.q: What is one benefit of social media?
What is one problem it can cause?
qs.a: Stress from comparing yourself to others.
Taking breaks and focusing on real-life activities.

reading.text: Motivation is not a constant resource; it fluctuates. People often wait to feel motivated before they start, but action can create motivation. Setting small goals and tracking progress can turn effort into a habit over time.

While technology has democratized access to information, it has also blurred the line between expertise and opinion. To navigate this landscape, individuals must cultivate critical thinking and evaluate sources with healthy skepticism.

qs.a: The line between expertise and opinion.

writingPrompts.A1.1: Write 5 simple sentences about yourself. (Name, age, country, school, hobby).
writingPrompts.A1.2: Write 8 sentences about your day (morning, school, evening).
writingPrompts.A2.1: Write 10–12 sentences about your weekend. Use time words (on Saturday, in the evening).
writingPrompts.A2.2: Write 12–15 sentences about a problem you had and how you solved it.
writingPrompts.B1.1: Write 150 words: Should school start later? Give reasons and an example.
writingPrompts.B1.2: Write 170 words: Social media is good and bad. Explain both sides and your opinion.
writingPrompts.B2: Write 200 words: How can habits change your life? Use examples.
writingPrompts.C1: Write 220 words: Evaluate the impact of technology on knowledge and opinion.

modules.title: Prepositions: IN / ON / AT
modules.desc: Träna IN/ON/AT med förståelse, fix-the-mistake, bygg meningar, reading och writing.

rules.heading: IN
rules.text: Countries, cities, years, months, long periods (in Sweden, in 2012, in July).

rules.heading: ON
rules.text: Days and dates (on Monday, on 5th May).

rules.heading: AT
rules.text: Exact time and specific points/places (at 3 o'clock, at the bus stop).

parts.title: Understand the rule
content: Tips: tänk IN (inne i), ON (på en dag), AT (en punkt).

items.bad: I'm at home on the evening.
items.good: I'm at home in the evening.

items.example: I will be home at 3 o'clock.
He waits at the bus stop.

parts.title: Mini reading (A2–B1)
parts.text: Emma lives in London. She works at a bank near the river. She was born in 1998. On Mondays, she starts work at 8:30. In the evening, she studies English at home. At the weekend, she meets friends in the city.

questions.q: What time does she start work on Mondays?
questions.a: She starts work at 8:30 on Mondays.
questions.hint: Use 'at' for time and 'on' for day.

questions.q: Where does she study in the evening?
questions.a: She studies English at home in the evening.

parts.title: Writing task (30–45 min)
parts.prompt:
Write 10 sentences about your life. Use:
• 3 sentences with IN
• 3 sentences with ON
• 3 sentences with AT
• 1 free sentence

Try to write about real things (home, school, hobbies, times, days).
checklist: I used AT correctly (exact times/specific places).
My sentences have capital letters and full stops.

parts.title: Reflection (5–10 min)
qs: What was the easiest rule today?
Write one example sentence for IN, ON and AT.

modules.title: Svenska översättningsfällor
modules.desc: Sluta översätta ord för ord. Träna vanliga fraser + skriv egna bättre meningar.

parts.title: Mini-lesson: Don’t translate word-for-word
content: Många svenska fraser låter konstiga på engelska om du översätter direkt.
Lösning: lär dig EN naturlig engelsk fras (chunk) i taget.
Regel: välj hellre en enkel naturlig mening än en avancerad direktöversättning.

items.q: Hur säger man 'Jag ska gå och lägga mig' bäst?
opts: I will go and lay me
I will go and put myself
Can I loan you your pen?

items.bad: I will go and lay me.

items.example: I'm going to take a shower.

parts.title: Mini reading (chunks)
parts.text: Ali is tired today. He says: 'How is it with you?' His friend laughs and answers: 'I'm good! How are you?' Ali learns a new rule: don’t translate. In the evening, he is cold, so he says: 'I'm cold.' Then he goes to bed early.

questions.q: What is the correct question to ask a friend?

questions.a: He learns not to translate word-for-word.

questions.q: What does he say when he is cold?
When does he go to bed?
questions.a: He goes to bed in the evening / early.
Example: How are you? / I'm cold.

parts.title: Writing task (30–45 min)
parts.prompt:
Write a short story (8–12 sentences) about a day where someone makes 3 Swedish-English mistakes and then fixes them. Use at least 3 chunks from today.

Checklist:
• 8–12 sentences
• 3 mistakes + corrections
• 3 chunks used correctly
checklist: I showed 3 mistakes and fixed them.
My story has capital letters and full stops.

parts.title: Reflection
qs: What mistake do you make most often?
Write your personal 'rule' for avoiding translation.
